file_name: SPIENERGYCO,LTD_07_10_2014-EX-10-Cooperation Agreement of 50MWp Photovoltaic Grid-connected Power Generation Project in Yangqiao of~1.txt

perturbation.original_text: Party B will register a foreign-funded company in Fenyi County before implementing the project; Party A will offer all the preferential policies to the company as per local investment promotion policies for foreign investment introduction.
perturbation.changed_text: Party B may, at its sole discretion, register a foreign-funded company in Fenyi County before implementing the project; Party A will offer preferential policies to the company as per local investment promotion policies for foreign investment introduction, subject to Party A's approval and ongoing economic conditions.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces uncertainty regarding the registration of the foreign-funded company. The original text mandates registration ('will register'), while the modified text makes it discretionary ('may, at its sole discretion'). Furthermore, the added condition 'subject to Party A's approval and ongoing economic conditions' creates additional ambiguity, contradicting the initial commitment of 'all the preferential policies' and thus affecting the enforcement of the original promise. This contradicts section V, where it was previously stated Party B 'will register'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Party B will register a foreign-funded company in Fenyi County before implementing the project; Party A will offer all the preferential policies to the company as per local investment promotion policies for foreign investment introduction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by making the registration of the foreign-funded company discretionary ('may, at its sole discretion') and adding a condition ('subject to Party A's approval and ongoing economic conditions'), which conflicts with the original text and the contradicted text, both of which state that 'Party B will register' the company. This creates uncertainty and impacts the enforceability of the preferential policies, directly contradicting the initial commitment. This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' because the clause is now located in multiple locations with conflicting implications, creating uncertainty about the obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Party A supports and guarantees the project construction and development of Party B, and will provide the most preferential policies and all- round service in the aspects of preliminary work, construction and grid-connected of the project, and actively coordinate relevant departments and units to help Party B accelerate project construction progress.
perturbation.changed_text: Party A supports the project construction and development of Party B to the extent feasible, and will endeavor to provide preferential policies and all-round service in the aspects of preliminary work, construction, and grid-connected of the project, while retaining full regulatory authority and discretion over project approvals. Party A will coordinate with relevant departments, but is not responsible for accelerating project construction progress.
perturbation.explanation: The modification introduces ambiguity by changing 'supports and guarantees' to 'supports to the extent feasible' and 'will provide' to 'will endeavor to provide'. The addition of 'while retaining full regulatory authority and discretion over project approvals' undermines the initial guarantee of support and the provision of preferential policies, making their enforcement uncertain. This contradicts the original intent of section I. By removing the active role of helping Party B accelerate project construction progress, the text is creating a lack of certainty on the progress, hence creating an In-Text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Party A supports and guarantees the project construction and development of Party B, and will provide the most preferential policies and all- round service in the aspects of preliminary work, construction and grid-connected of the project, and actively coordinate relevant departments and units to help Party B accelerate project construction progress.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly weakens Party A's obligations and introduces ambiguity, directly contradicting the original firm guarantees and support. The original text states Party A 'supports and guarantees' and 'will provide,' creating a clear commitment. The changed text modifies this to 'supports to the extent feasible' and 'will endeavor to provide,' which are conditional and less binding. The addition of 'retaining full regulatory authority and discretion' further undermines the initial guarantee, making the provision of preferential policies uncertain. This contradicts the original text, which promises preferential policies without qualification, and the active coordination to accelerate project construction. By changing the active role of helping to accelerate the project, a contradiction is created, which is in direct conflict with the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Party B promises that land nature will not be changed for building large-scale PV grid-connected power generation project in the plot; comprehensive development will be carried out according to relevant national stipulations, such commercial crops will be interplanted as agriculture and forestry as well as medicinal materials; local employees will enjoy the priority to be employed; local building materials will be adopted and relevant expenses will be paid on schedule.
perturbation.changed_text: Party B intends to maintain the existing land nature where feasible for building the PV project; Comprehensive development will be attempted to be carried out according to relevant national stipulations; Preference will be given to local employees and suppliers, but Party B is not required to prioritize them over other qualified candidates/suppliers. Expenses will be paid on schedule, assuming sufficient cash flow.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing 'promises that land nature will not be changed' with 'intends to maintain the existing land nature where feasible,' the guarantee regarding land use is weakened. Likewise, 'comprehensive development will be carried out' changes to 'will be attempted to be carried out', 'local employees will enjoy the priority' changes to 'Preference will be given', and 'expenses will be paid on schedule' is modified to 'Expenses will be paid on schedule, assuming sufficient cash flow' all add uncertainty. The original section IV provided assurance and defined specific obligations, whereas the modified version introduces significant ambiguity, undermining the original agreement and creating an in-text contradiction within the enforcement of the original obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Party B promises that land nature will not be changed for building large-scale PV grid-connected power generation project in the plot; comprehensive development will be carried out according to relevant national stipulations, such commercial crops will be interplanted as agriculture and forestry as well as medicinal materials; local employees will enjoy the priority to be employed; local building materials will be adopted and relevant expenses will be paid on schedule.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the promises made in the original text, creating a direct conflict. For example, 'Party B promises that land nature will not be changed' is replaced with 'Party B intends to maintain the existing land nature where feasible.' This introduces ambiguity and uncertainty where the contradicted text provides a clear guarantee. Similarly, the change from 'local employees will enjoy the priority to be employed' to 'Preference will be given to local employees' weakens the obligation. This directly contradicts the firm commitments made elsewhere in the document, creating an in-text contradiction.
